text:
King's College Hospital 

Department of Neurosciences 

Clinic Date: 04 January 2022 

Dr Priya Sharma
Consultant Neurologist  
King's College Hospital  
Department of Neurosciences, King's College Hospital, London SE5 9RS  

Dear Dr Priya Sharma
Consultant Neurologist,  

Daniel Carter, DOB: 14-03-1985, Hospital No: KCH829104 NHS No. 9473105628  
12 Brook Mews, London, SE15 4BQ

Thank you for asking me to review your patient. He attended today in a settled state and we were able to have a clear discussion. He reports that he has been Seizure-free since 2 July 2021, and there have been no witnessed episodes by family or colleagues during this period. He commutes mainly by bicycle and notes no events or near-misses, with day-to-day activities unchanged from baseline.

From the available history and prior investigations, there is no current evidence to support a diagnosis of epilepsy. Earlier episodes previously labelled as “seizures” appear to have resolved, and he has not required antiseizure medication for several months. On balance, my impression is that he does not have epilepsy at present. We discussed this openly, including the importance of continuing routine care and monitoring symptoms should anything new arise; he understood and agreed with the plan. There are no nocturnal events, no post-event confusion, and no injuries reported.

Neurological examination today was unremarkable. Cognitive function, speech, and gait were within normal limits. He reports stable mood and good sleep. No psychotic or dissociative phenomena were described. Given sustained remission of episodes and lack of features suggestive of an epileptic disorder, I have advised that no further epilepsy-specific investigations are required at this time.

I will discharge him from the epilepsy pathway with the understanding that he can be re-referred if new symptoms develop. 

Yours sincerely

entities.text: He reports that he has been Seizure-free since 2 July 2021

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states: "He reports that he has been Seizure-free since 2 July 2021." The clinic date is 04 January 2022. Calculate duration from 2 July 2021 to 4 January 2022: July 2 to August 2 = 1 month; to September 2 = 2 months; to October 2 = 3 months; to November 2 = 4 months; to December 2 = 5 months; to January 2 = 6 months; plus 2 additional days. Therefore, the patient has been seizure-free for at least 6 months at the time of the clinic visit, meeting the criterion for 'seizure free for <value> <month>'.
